---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: a42da2204dace5c092d2aae1610b1c5d94cfb7aa
findings:
  - { id: judged-illustrative-tier-vs-body-figure-conflation, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 and dc-3 treat 'illustrative' as exclusively the body-figure class (parent dc-8: discovered only in the current spec's body, rendered as undispositioned context, never a finding), but parent dc-3 defines illustrative as a per-artifact verification COVERAGE tier that a first-class class:proposal artifact can hold (no generator), and parent dc-1 makes that tier reachable by fail-closed downgrade when extraction fails. An accepted proposal that has fallen to illustrative coverage is simultaneously in-scope for every alignment run with a promised computed Finding folding regeneration-divergence deltas (story dc-1 + dc-3) and declared 'NOT findings' (story dc-3) — and the divergence deltas dc-3 promises cannot be computed for it. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-coverage-disclosure-dropped-from-finding, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-3 requires verification coverage (full/partial/illustrative) to be three-valued and 'disclosed per artifact'. Story dc-3 specifies Finding.Text carries only the divergence deltas and candidate witness, with no coverage-tier disclosure, so a partial-coverage proposal (flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary) yields a finding indistinguishable from a fully-verified one — silently presenting partial verification as full, contrary to the parent's disclosure decision, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-schema-enumeration-omits-ratified-fields, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1's load-bearing premise enumerates the ratified 02 proposal schema as 'id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from only', but parent dc-7 ratified base-digest and the illustrative marker in the same amendment batch (and story dc-2 itself relies on the base digest). The 'only' claim misstates the ratified surface the corpus-wide-scoping argument is derived from; if the omitted fields matter to scoping (e.g. the illustrative marker), the smallest-invention reading in dc-1 rests on an incomplete schema characterization, undeclared against parent dc-7. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/alignment-section#dc-1, spec/alignment-section#dc-2, spec/alignment-section#dc-3, spec/alignment-section#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"illustrative-tier-vs-body-figure-conflation\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 and dc-3 treat 'illustrative' as exclusively the body-figure class (parent dc-8: discovered only in the current spec's body, rendered as undispositioned context, never a finding), but parent dc-3 defines illustrative as a per-artifact verification COVERAGE tier that a first-class class:proposal artifact can hold (no generator), and parent dc-1 makes that tier reachable by fail-closed downgrade when extraction fails. An accepted proposal that has fallen to illustrative coverage is simultaneously in-scope for every alignment run with a promised computed Finding folding regeneration-divergence deltas (story dc-1 + dc-3) and declared 'NOT findings' (story dc-3) — and the divergence deltas dc-3 promises cannot be computed for it. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"coverage-disclosure-dropped-from-finding\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-3 requires verification coverage (full/partial/illustrative) to be three-valued and 'disclosed per artifact'. Story dc-3 specifies Finding.Text carries only the divergence deltas and candidate witness, with no coverage-tier disclosure, so a partial-coverage proposal (flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary) yields a finding indistinguishable from a fully-verified one — silently presenting partial verification as full, contrary to the parent's disclosure decision, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"schema-enumeration-omits-ratified-fields\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1's load-bearing premise enumerates the ratified 02 proposal schema as 'id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from only', but parent dc-7 ratified base-digest and the illustrative marker in the same amendment batch (and story dc-2 itself relies on the base digest). The 'only' claim misstates the ratified surface the corpus-wide-scoping argument is derived from; if the omitted fields matter to scoping (e.g. the illustrative marker), the smallest-invention reading in dc-1 rests on an incomplete schema characterization, undeclared against parent dc-7.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/alignment-section@a42da2204dace5c092d2aae1610b1c5d94cfb7aa], digest: sha256:142a050ad7a76f0a96331ed5193562441a7778ac122208e05a6fd83b0949bfe4, integrity: sha256:20f9af7ac0ca96685a751f0781a574d36ff654656634b2b758d94702242c6452 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-illustrative-tier-vs-body-figure-conflation** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 and dc-3 treat 'illustrative' as exclusively the body-figure class (parent dc-8: discovered only in the current spec's body, rendered as undispositioned context, never a finding), but parent dc-3 defines illustrative as a per-artifact verification COVERAGE tier that a first-class class:proposal artifact can hold (no generator), and parent dc-1 makes that tier reachable by fail-closed downgrade when extraction fails. An accepted proposal that has fallen to illustrative coverage is simultaneously in-scope for every alignment run with a promised computed Finding folding regeneration-divergence deltas (story dc-1 + dc-3) and declared 'NOT findings' (story dc-3) — and the divergence deltas dc-3 promises cannot be computed for it. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-coverage-disclosure-dropped-from-finding** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Parent dc-3 requires verification coverage (full/partial/illustrative) to be three-valued and 'disclosed per artifact'. Story dc-3 specifies Finding.Text carries only the divergence deltas and candidate witness, with no coverage-tier disclosure, so a partial-coverage proposal (flowchart beyond the generator's vocabulary) yields a finding indistinguishable from a fully-verified one — silently presenting partial verification as full, contrary to the parent's disclosure decision, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared. (confidence 0.45)
- **judged-schema-enumeration-omits-ratified-fields** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1's load-bearing premise enumerates the ratified 02 proposal schema as 'id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from only', but parent dc-7 ratified base-digest and the illustrative marker in the same amendment batch (and story dc-2 itself relies on the base digest). The 'only' claim misstates the ratified surface the corpus-wide-scoping argument is derived from; if the omitted fields matter to scoping (e.g. the illustrative marker), the smallest-invention reading in dc-1 rests on an incomplete schema characterization, undeclared against parent dc-7. (confidence 0.30)
